title: Nebius co-founder Roman Chernin argues that falling AI costs will ...
search_path_label: A 级媒体 / GDELT 路径
full_text:
Tech in Asia's Post
Tech in Asia
18h ·
Nebius co-founder Roman Chernin argues that falling AI costs will expand adoption, driving greater demand for infrastructure and cloud computing.
Roman Chernin of Nebius argues cheaper AI encourages wider adoption, increasing demand for computing power, as customers spend budgets to use the technology on more tasks.
The release of the DeepSeek model sent Nebius to experience a 40% drop in its stock price, yet it was “the best week in sales” as companies ran inference in production workloads.
Scroll down to see how Nebius Token Factory lets companies run standard or fine-tuned open-source models without worrying about backend maintenance.
Want to keep up with the ins and outs of the #tech ecosystem in Asia? Sign up for free at techin.asia/newsletters
techinasia.com
Why falling AI costs spark investor panic but drive new revenue
